name: AWS Elemental MediaConvert
description: AWS managed video transcoding — submit a job that reads an input from S3 and writes outputs to S3 (HLS, DASH, MP4, framecaps, etc.). Effectively ffmpeg-as-a-service at AWS scale, billed per output minute. Authenticates via SigV4 with an IAM access key + secret. The job's encoding settings live under `settings.outputGroups[]` and follow the MediaConvert OutputGroup schema.

credential_fields.name: access_key_id
credential_fields.label: AWS Access Key ID
credential_fields.description: IAM access key with mediaconvert:* and the s3:Get/Put permissions needed for input + output buckets. The MediaConvert service-linked role takes care of S3 access during the job itself, but the API caller still needs mediaconvert:CreateJob etc.

credential_fields.name: region
credential_fields.label: AWS Region
credential_fields.description: Region where MediaConvert is enabled for your account (us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, etc.). MediaConvert is now globally addressable via regional endpoints; you no longer need to call DescribeEndpoints first. The deprecated account-specific endpoint still works if you set this credential to that hostname instead — see the docs.

credential_fields.name: session_token
credential_fields.label: AWS Session Token
credential_fields.description: Required only when using temporary STS credentials.

tools.name: create_job
tools.description: Submit a transcoding job. Reads source(s) from S3, writes output(s) to S3 paths configured in `settings.outputGroups[].outputGroupSettings.<groupType>Settings.destination`. Returns immediately with the job id; poll get_job until status=COMPLETE. Settings are MediaConvert's full schema — for simple transcodes pass {settings: {inputs: [{fileInput: "s3://..."}], outputGroups: [{outputGroupSettings: {type: "FILE_GROUP_SETTINGS", fileGroupSettings: {destination: "s3://..."}}, outputs: [{containerSettings: {container: "MP4"}, videoDescription: {codecSettings: {codec: "H_264"}}, audioDescriptions: [{codecSettings: {codec: "AAC"}}]}]}]}}.

role.description: ARN of the IAM role MediaConvert assumes to read input + write output. Must trust mediaconvert.amazonaws.com and have S3 access. e.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MediaConvertRole.

settings.description: Job settings — a deeply nested object describing inputs and output groups. Required keys: `inputs` (array of {fileInput, audioSelectors?, videoSelector?, ...}), `outputGroups` (array of {outputGroupSettings, outputs[]}). See MediaConvert reference for the full schema.

queue.description: Queue ARN. Omit for the account default. Use a dedicated queue for high-priority work to bypass the shared queue.

priority.description: Higher runs sooner within a queue. Default 0.

clientRequestToken.description: Idempotency token. Resubmitting with the same token returns the original job rather than creating a duplicate.

jobTemplate.description: Name of a saved JobTemplate to inherit settings from. Lets you skip most of `settings`. Templates are managed in the MediaConvert console.

accelerationSettings.description: {mode: ENABLED | PREFERRED | DISABLED}. ENABLED uses GPU acceleration (faster, more expensive); PREFERRED falls back to CPU when GPU is unavailable.

tags.description: Free-form key/value pairs for cost allocation.

userMetadata.description: Echoed in CloudWatch events. Good for round-tripping the caller's render_id.

tools.name: get_job
tools.description: Fetch one job's status + outputs. Status enum: SUBMITTED, PROGRESSING, COMPLETE, CANCELED, ERROR. When COMPLETE, the response includes `outputGroupDetails[]` with the output S3 URIs.

tools.name: list_jobs
tools.description: Paginate jobs in this account. Filter by status / queue / order.

nextToken.description: Pagination cursor from the previous response.

tools.name: cancel_job
tools.description: Request cancellation. Sends PUT /jobs/{id} with body {status: CANCEL_REQUESTED}. The job moves to CANCELED once MediaConvert acks; in-flight encoding work stops and you stop being billed for further minutes.

tools.name: describe_endpoints
tools.description: Returns the account-specific endpoint URL. Historically required (you HAD to use that URL for everything else); now MediaConvert routes through regional endpoints by default and this is mostly informational. Useful for legacy accounts or when troubleshooting.

tools.name: list_queues
tools.description: List MediaConvert queues in this region. Each queue is a billing + scheduling unit; jobs can target a specific queue or fall back to the account default.

tools.name: list_job_templates
tools.description: List saved JobTemplates. Templates encode reusable settings; passing `jobTemplate` on create_job inherits from one.
